summary: Provision new Application Auto Scaling targets and target-tracking policies for ECS services. Every stand-up routes to an engineer for review — other service namespaces and day-2 operations land in a later wave.

operations.title: Provision a new Application Auto Scaling target
operations.summary: Request a brand-new scaling target for an ECS service; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Register a new scalable target as a complete specification: capacity bounds and the ECS service it scales. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: target_name
params.label: Target name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names this target in the generated draft — a scalable target has no name field of its own.

params.name: service_namespace
params.label: Service namespace
params.help: Scoped to ECS service scaling — the common case; other namespaces (DynamoDB, RDS, Spot Fleet, SageMaker…) are an engineer decision via the catch-all request.

params.name: scalable_dimension
params.label: Scalable dimension
params.help: The one ECS scaling dimension — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_id
params.label: ECS scaling target id
params.help: The form service/<cluster name>/<service name>, matching the ECS cluster and service being scaled.

params.name: min_capacity
params.label: Minimum capacity
params.help: Fewest tasks the service is ever scaled down to.

params.name: max_capacity
params.label: Maximum capacity
params.help: Most tasks the service may scale up to.

params.name: role_arn
params.label: IAM role
params.help: Leave blank to use the AWS service-linked role for ECS scaling.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this scaling target does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this scaling target.

decisions: Registering a target for a non-ECS namespace (DynamoDB, RDS, Spot Fleet, SageMaker, custom resources) is an engineer decision — use the request-a-change catch-all
Suspended-state overrides (pausing scale-in/scale-out/scheduled scaling) are an engineer follow-up after creation

operations.title: Provision a new Application Auto Scaling policy
operations.summary: Request a brand-new target-tracking scaling policy for an ECS service; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new target-tracking scaling policy as a complete specification: the metric it tracks and the scaling target it attaches to. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: policy_name
params.label: Policy name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like tt-cpu-60. Names the policy in AWS and in the generated draft.

params.name: policy_type
params.label: Policy type
params.help: Target tracking is the common case — step scaling and predictive scaling are engineer decisions.

params.name: resource_id
params.label: ECS scaling target id
params.help: The existing scaling target this policy attaches to: service/<cluster name>/<service name>, matching an already-registered aws_appautoscaling_target.

params.name: scalable_dimension
params.label: Scalable dimension
params.help: Matches the ECS scaling target above — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: service_namespace
params.label: Service namespace
params.help: Scoped to ECS service scaling — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: predefined_metric_type
params.label: Metric
params.help: The built-in ECS metric this policy targets.

params.name: target_value
params.label: Target value
params.help: The target percentage the metric should hover around.

params.name: scale_out_cooldown
params.label: Scale-out cooldown (seconds)
params.help: Seconds to wait after a scale-out before another scale-out can start.

params.name: scale_in_cooldown
params.label: Scale-in cooldown (seconds)
params.help: Seconds to wait after a scale-in before another scale-in can start.

params.name: disable_scale_in
params.label: Disable scale-in
params.help: On prevents this policy from ever scaling capacity down — scale-in stays a manual/other-policy decision.

decisions: Step scaling and predictive scaling policy types are engineer decisions — this form covers the common target-tracking case
A customized CloudWatch metric (customized_metric_specification) instead of a predefined ECS metric is an engineer decision
This resource carries no tags attribute in the provider schema — it is not taggable
